analysis: That first night on 1 July was rough — only 6 hours 9 minutes with nearly two hours spent awake — but you clearly sorted something out, because nights two and three came back swinging with 7h 34m and a genuinely gorgeous 7h 56m, and that 2h 34m of REM on night three is the kind of number that makes me want to throw a party. You've held steady in the 7-hour range for most of the stretch, which is not nothing — that's your wind-down stack doing real work. The one thing I'm watching is your deep sleep, which has been sitting mostly in the 28–36 minute window across the middle of the week — those two bookend nights with 51 and 53 minutes of deep show you can get there, so whatever you did differently on nights one and six, it's worth thinking about.
